   <item>
      <title>Bob Myers Emerges As Most Frequently Mentioned Mavericks GM Candidate</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284825/Bob-Myers-Emerges-As-Most-Frequently-Mentioned-Mavericks-GM-Candidate</link>
      <description><p>Former Golden State Warriors president Bob Myers has surfaced as the most commonly cited target in the general manager search of the Dallas Mavericks, according to league sources. Myers currently serves as president of Harris Blitzer Sports & Entertainment, overseeing consulting roles across multiple pro sports franchises.</p>
<p>Myers has already declined overtures from the Phoenix Suns, Atlanta Hawks and undoubtedly other NBA teams since departing Golden State following the 2022-23 season. His HBSE role, which spans the Philadelphia 76ers, New Jersey Devils, Crystal Palace FC and the Washington Commanders, is widely credited with drawing him away from ESPN.</p>
<p>Mavericks CEO Rick Welts, who worked alongside Myers during Golden State's dynasty years, would serve as a natural advocate for the hire.</p>
<p>Owner Patrick Dumont has set an ambitious tone for the search. Names like Sam Presti, R.C. Buford and Brad Stevens have been floated, though Dallas privately acknowledges those pursuits are unrealistic given the strength of those executive-franchise bonds.</p>
<p>Tim Connelly is also believed to be of strong interest to Dallas if he departs the Minnesota Timberwolves after this season. The Wolves, however, are said to be working toward a long-term extension with Connelly following back-to-back Western Conference finals appearances.</p>
<p>Free agent executive Masai Ujiri, a two-time Executive of the Year and the architect of the Toronto Raptors 2019 championship, remains the most accomplished available candidate, though he recently joined the WNBA's Toronto Tempo ownership group.</p>
<p>Co-interim GMs Matt Riccardi and Michael Finley are also expected to be considered. Dallas's search is not expected to begin formally until after the regular season concludes April 12.</p>
<p>"It's the most important decision that the organization is going to make for the coming seasons," Welts said Tuesday. "We are going to take our time and get it right."</p>
<p>While the Mavericks have a franchise player in Cooper Flagg to make the front office job attractive, <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Dallas-Mavericks/6/draft-picks">they do not control their draft picks from 2027 until 2030</a>. But if the Mavericks end up with <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/lottery_simulator">a top-4 pick at this year's lottery</a>, they could then add another potential All-Star to pair with Flagg.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 12:04:49 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284825/Bob-Myers-Emerges-As-Most-Frequently-Mentioned-Mavericks-GM-Candidate</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Myers_Rob_gsw_170311.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Mavericks Want Jason Kidd To Remain As Head Coach Amid GM Search</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284827/Mavericks-Want-Jason-Kidd-To-Remain-As-Head-Coach-Amid-GM-Search</link>
      <description><p>The Dallas Mavericks want Jason Kidd to remain as head coach and have made finding a general manager who can partner with him a central priority, according to league sources. The franchise is focused on building around rookie Cooper Flagg following the February 2025 trade of Luka Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers.</p>
<p>Reports suggesting Kidd might pursue a front office role have been consistently dismissed by sources close to the organization. Kidd was a target of the New York Knicks last offseason, but the Mavericks resisted the overtures and <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/282295/Jason-Kidd-Mavericks-Sign-Multi-Year-Extension">signed him to a multi-year extension in October of last year</a>.</p>
<p>Kidd has been a stabilizing force for Dallas since his 2021 hiring, which coincided with Nico Harrison's arrival as general manager under then-owner Mark Cuban. He guided the Mavericks to the 2022 Western Conference Finals in his first season and returned the franchise to the NBA Finals in 2024.</p>
<p>Harrison was dismissed in November 2025, nine months after the Doncic trade triggered significant fan backlash. Co-interim GMs Matt Riccardi and Michael Finley have since managed basketball operations and are expected to be considered for the permanent post.</p>
<p>CEO Rick Welts stressed Tuesday that incoming leadership must share a clear vision for constructing a competitive roster around Flagg.</p>
<p>"It's the most important decision that the organization is going to make for the coming seasons," Welts said. "We are going to take our time and get it right."</p>
<p>Dallas's formal search is not expected to launch until after the regular season ends April 12, with ownership targeting a hire before the late-June draft.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 12:15:08 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284827/Mavericks-Want-Jason-Kidd-To-Remain-As-Head-Coach-Amid-GM-Search</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Kidd_Jason_dal_240606.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Zion Williamson Expected To Be Put On Trade Block By Pelicans In Offseason</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284837/Zion-Williamson-Expected-To-Be-Put-On-Trade-Block-By-Pelicans-In-Offseason</link>
      <description><p>Multiple executives around the NBA expect the New Orleans Pelicans to listen to trade offers on Zion Williamson, who becomes extension-eligible this offseason. The two-time All-Star says he is aware of the speculation and acknowledges the business reality he faces.</p>
<p>"New Orleans is home for me. It's where I want to be," Williamson told Yahoo Sports. "But at the end of the day, if we're going to be realistic about it, the NBA is a business. I could be traded in the offseason, or I could be traded before [next season's] trade deadline. Not that I want that to happen. But that's just the realism of it."</p>
<p>The trade chatter stems largely from a front office decision made nine months ago. Joe Dumars and Troy Weaver surrendered the 23rd pick and <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/future_drafts/detailed">an unprotected 2026 first-rounder</a> to move up to 13th in the draft and select Maryland forward Derik Queen, a poor defensive player whose fit alongside Williamson has proven deeply problematic.</p>
<p>Interim coach James Borrego has largely abandoned pairing the two after dismal early-season results. The Pelicans went 15-41 before the All-Star break but have posted a 10-10 record since separating the combination.</p>
<p>The unprotected 2026 first-rounder owed to Atlanta currently carries a 32% chance of landing in the top four of what scouts consider a strong draft class, raising the stakes of the Queen gamble considerably.</p>
<p>Williamson, who turns 26 in July, ranks among the league's most productive isolation scorers this season and has stayed largely healthy. However, skepticism persists leaguewide given his injury history, limited defensive impact and a contract guaranteeing $42.2 million next season.</p>
<p>"Right now he's a borderline All-Star who makes a lot of money," one Western Conference executive said. "That's the sort of player a lot of teams are now avoiding."</p>
<p>Sources close to Williamson say neither he nor his representatives have received any indication from New Orleans that a move is imminent.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 15:12:43 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284837/Zion-Williamson-Expected-To-Be-Put-On-Trade-Block-By-Pelicans-In-Offseason</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Williamson_Zion_nop_240325.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Tom Izzo Gave Serious Consideration To Taking Suns Head Coaching Job</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284836/Tom-Izzo-Gave-Serious-Consideration-To-Taking-Suns-Head-Coaching-Job</link>
      <description><p>Tom Izzo was offered the head coaching position of the Phoenix Suns last offseason. The job eventually went to Jordan Ott, who worked under Izzo for several years at Michigan State before eventually moving to the NBA.</p>
<p>Izzo decided to remain at Michigan State and led the school to the Sweet 16.</p>
<p>Izzo had previously been pursued for head coaching jobs by the Cleveland Cavaliers, Atlanta Hawks, Chicago Bulls and Brooklyn Nets.</p>
<p>Suns governor Mat Ishbia played for Izzo in college and remains closely tied to the program. Also, Phoenix general manager Brian Gregory was an assistant coach under Izzo when the Spartans won their national championship in 2000.</p>
<p>"I've had more than a couple job offers in the NBA, looked at one last year with Phoenix - my former player Mat Ishbia," said Izzo. "That was hard, that was a hard thing to turn down... we talked seriously about it."</p>
<p>Ott has the Suns firmly in the playoff picture in the Western Conference and already well over their over/under wins projection of 31.5</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 14:53:27 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284836/Tom-Izzo-Gave-Serious-Consideration-To-Taking-Suns-Head-Coaching-Job</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Izzo_Tom_ncaa_090406.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA Fines Blazers $100,000, Suspends Two Executives For Illegal Contact With Yang Hansen</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284842/NBA-Fines-Blazers-$100000-Suspends-Two-Executives-For-Illegal-Contact-With-Yang-Hansen</link>
      <description><p> </p>
<p>The NBA fined the Portland Trail Blazers $100,000 on Wednesday and suspended two team executives for improper contact made with center Yang Hansen in December 2023, nearly two years before the 7-foot Chinese big man was eligible for the draft.</p>
<p>Assistant general managers Mike Schmitz and Sergi Oliva each received two-week unpaid suspensions. The league ruled that both the franchise and the two officials violated rules governing contact with draft-ineligible players.</p>
<p>Portland self-reported the violation and cooperated fully with the league's investigation.</p>
<p>"When this was brought to our attention the Portland Trail Blazers self-reported to the NBA," the team said in a statement. "The team cooperated fully with the investigation and accept the league's determination."</p>
<p>Hansen was selected 16th overall by the Memphis Grizzlies in the 2025 draft. Portland acquired his rights the same night via a draft-night trade. Prior to entering the NBA, Hansen spent two seasons with the Qingdao Eagles in China's top professional league.</p>
<p>The 21-year-old has appeared in 41 games as a rookie this season for the Blazers, averaging 2.3 points and 1.6 rebounds in 7.2 minutes per contest.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 22:33:40 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284842/NBA-Fines-Blazers-$100000-Suspends-Two-Executives-For-Illegal-Contact-With-Yang-Hansen</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Hansen_Yang_por_251026.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Draymond Green Unlikely To Enter Free Agency This Offseason</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284820/Draymond-Green-Unlikely-To-Enter-Free-Agency-This-Offseason</link>
      <description><p>Golden State Warriors forward Draymond Green knows he's entering the twilight of his NBA career. Green also recognizes that comes with some uncertainty, including potentially being traded. But Green has some control over his immediate future, because he holds a player option for next season.</p>
<p>Green is expected to pick up his $27.7 million player option for the 2026-27 season. If Green opts out, it's expected to be to sign a longer-term deal, which would lessen his cap hit starting with next season.</p>
<p>The veteran forward also understands that he's hit the point of his career where he could be traded. Green said being mentioned in trade rumors for Giannis Antetokounmpo stung initially, but that he quickly came to see it as a positive.</p>
<p>"I'm a human being," Green said. "There was a second of me [at the deadline] that felt like, 'Damn, they really going to do that to me?'</p>
<p>"I understand this business probably [as] good as any player. I understand like, yo, this is Giannis Antetokounmpo. They not just about to trade me for a bag of peanuts. It would've almost been a point of pride."</p>
<p>As always, Green was direct about the idea of being traded to the Milwaukee Bucks.</p>
<p>"I'll be honest though. I didn't want to play for Milwaukee," Green said.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 09:37:12 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284820/Draymond-Green-Unlikely-To-Enter-Free-Agency-This-Offseason</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Green_Draymond_gsw_230311.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>New Blazers Owner Tom Dundon Sets Higher Standards, Addresses Arena Future, Front Office</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284824/New-Blazers-Owner-Tom-Dundon-Sets-Higher-Standards-Addresses-Arena-Future-Front-Office</link>
      <description><p>Tom Dundon delivered a direct message to Portland Trail Blazers players on his first day as owner Tuesday that the franchise's current trajectory falls short of his expectations. The meeting took place inside Intuit Dome prior to Portland's game against the LA Clippers.</p>
<p>"Right now, where we are isn't where I think we can be," Dundon told the team.</p>
<p>Dundon, who also owns the NHL's Carolina Hurricanes, built a winning culture through a combination of analytics and instinct. Carolina has reached the playoffs in seven straight seasons and advanced to the Eastern Conference finals three times under his ownership.</p>
<p>He inherits a Blazers franchise that hasn't reached the playoffs since 2021 and has seen declining attendance throughout an ongoing rebuild. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/future_drafts/detailed">The Blazers control the Milwaukee Bucks' drafts in 2027, 2028, 2029</a>, which could potentially supercharge their roster.</p>
<p>In an exclusive interview with The Athletic, Dundon addressed staff concerns about potential sweeping personnel changes.</p>
<p>"I don't like getting rid of people. I like to see who's capable of taking that next step," he said. "They need to worry about what they're gonna do."</p>
<p>Dundon acknowledged he has not yet formed firm opinions on interim head coach Tiago Splitter or general manager Joe Cronin, saying he needs more information before making those calls.</p>
<p>On the Blazers' push for $600 million in Moda Center renovations, Dundon indicated he expects progress, citing a proposed 20-year commitment to Portland as the franchise's primary contribution. He dismissed speculation about relocation as unfounded, noting no internal discussions have taken place on the subject.</p>
<p>"I fully expect to get everything we're working on done," Dundon said.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 11:56:14 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284824/New-Blazers-Owner-Tom-Dundon-Sets-Higher-Standards-Addresses-Arena-Future-Front-Office</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_portlandtrailblazers.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Tristan Enaruna Wins G League Most Improved Player Award After Doubling Scoring Average</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284821/Tristan-Enaruna-Wins-G-League-Most-Improved-Player-Award-After-Doubling-Scoring-Average</link>
      <description><p>Cleveland Charge forward Tristan Enaruna has been named the 2025-26 <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/gleague/awards/by-type/Most-Improved-Player/4">NBA G League Most Improved Player</a>, the league announced Tuesday. The honor is determined by a vote of all 30 G League head coaches and general managers.</p>
<p>Enaruna appeared in 38 games this season, averaging 20.3 points on 54.8% shooting with 5.6 rebounds and 1.2 steals across 31.2 minutes per game. He earned a two-way call-up to the Cleveland Cavaliers on January 28.</p>
<p>The forward nearly doubled his scoring output from last season, climbing from 11.0 to 20.3 points per game. He also improved his shooting efficiency from 51.8% to 62.0%, his three-point percentage from 29.2% to 37.2% and his free-throw percentage from 58.8% to 75.0%.</p>
<p>Charge head coach Eli Kell-Abrams praised the recognition.</p>
<p>"This award is a huge testament to the daily work Tristan puts in, how intentional he's been with his development, and the growing confidence he continues to play with," Kell-Abrams said. "We could not be happier for T and we are excited for him to continue to grow during a long and successful career in this game."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/gleague/awards/by-type/Most-Improved-Player/4">Past winners of the award include Gabe Vincent and Lester Qunones</a>. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 11:35:11 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284821/Tristan-Enaruna-Wins-G-League-Most-Improved-Player-Award-After-Doubling-Scoring-Average</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/gleague_logo.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Bill Self Commits To Kansas For 2026-27 Season After Health Concerns</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284841/Bill-Self-Commits-To-Kansas-For-2026-27-Season-After-Health-Concerns</link>
      <description><p>Kansas head coach Bill Self announced Wednesday he will return to coach the Jayhawks next season, ending weeks of uncertainty surrounding his future following a series of health concerns. Self said the decision came "with renewed clarity and the ongoing support from our administration."</p>
<p>"I remain focused and committed to Kansas and competing for a National Championship," Self said in a statement. "I look forward to seeing and hearing the best fans in college basketball next season at Allen Fieldhouse."</p>
<p>Self, 63, spent considerable time this week consulting with those close to him before reaching his decision. His announcement arrives more than a week after he indicated he would discuss his coaching future with family following multiple medical episodes in recent years.</p>
<p>The Hall of Fame coach has required cardiac intervention on two separate occasions. In 2023, he underwent a heart catheterization and had two stents placed after being hospitalized with chest tightness and balance concerns, forcing him to miss the Big 12 and NCAA tournaments. Last July, he was hospitalized again after experiencing concerning symptoms and had two additional stents inserted before being released two days later. In January, he missed a road game at Colorado after being taken to the hospital as a precaution.</p>
<p>Self acknowledged after Kansas's season-ending NCAA tournament loss to St. John's that a return was not guaranteed.</p>
<p>"I've had, obviously, some issues off the court healthwise. And that will be discussed," he said at the time. "But I love what I do. I want to feel good while I'm doing it, though."</p>
<p>His return carries considerable weight for the program and the sport. Self has compiled 855 wins across 33 seasons at Oral Roberts, Tulsa, Illinois and Kansas. Since arriving in Lawrence in 2003, he has won two national championships and reached four Final Fours.</p>
<p>The announcement arrives just days before the NCAA transfer portal opens Tuesday, the day after the national championship game.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 22:31:54 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284841/Bill-Self-Commits-To-Kansas-For-2026-27-Season-After-Health-Concerns</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Self_Bill_ncaa_080328.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Breanna Stewart, Sabrina Ionescu Both Announce Plans To Re-Sign With Liberty</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284840/Breanna-Stewart-Sabrina-Ionescu-Both-Announce-Plans-To-Re-Sign-With-Liberty</link>
      <description><p>Two-time WNBA MVP Breanna Stewart announced she will remain with the New York Liberty in free agency, keeping the league's most decorated roster largely intact heading into the 2026 season. Teammate Sabrina Ionescu separately confirmed her intention to re-sign with New York on Wednesday at USA Basketball training camp.</p>
<p>"I'm gonna just set the record straight here: I will be staying in New York," Stewart said on her podcast, "Game Recognize Game with Stewie and Myles." "I'm not planning on taking any free agency meetings, even though I am an unrestricted free agent. My family is set up here, we're solid here. I'm going to be back in New York and that's all there is to it."</p>
<p>Stewart, a seven-time All-Star and three-time Liberty champion, averaged 18.3 points, 6.5 rebounds, 3.5 assists and 1.4 steals in 2025 before battling injuries late in the season and playoffs. She went on to lead her Mist BC squad to a title at Unrivaled, the 3-on-3 league she co-founded with Napheesa Collier.</p>
<p>Ionescu, a four-time All-Star who averaged 18.2 points, 5.7 assists and 4.9 rebounds last season, expressed confidence in the team's direction under first-year head coach Chris DeMarco.</p>
<p>"Obviously I'm where I'm supposed to be," Ionescu said. "Never thought anything different, and so excited to be able to sign and get started."</p>
<p>The Liberty also hold negotiating rights to several other key free agents including Jonquel Jones, Natasha Cloud, Emma Meesseman, Kennedy Burke and Betnijah Laney-Hamilton.</p>
<p>The exact free agency timeline remains unconfirmed as the league's new collective bargaining agreement is still being finalized. The expansion draft is scheduled for Friday with training camp set to begin April 19, creating an unusually compressed decision window for players and teams leaguewide.</p>
<p>"It's going to be real quick-hitting, and there's obviously a lot of money on the table," Ionescu said. "I think people are also going to have that to consider and they didn't always have that."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 22:26:39 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284840/Breanna-Stewart-Sabrina-Ionescu-Both-Announce-Plans-To-Re-Sign-With-Liberty</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Stewart_Breanna_nyl_240522.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA's Gap Between Playoff, Lottery Teams Reaches Historic Levels Exposing Two-League Problems</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284839/NBAs-Gap-Between-Playoff-Lottery-Teams-Reaches-Historic-Levels-Exposing-Two-League-Problems</link>
      <description><p>The divide between playoff contenders and lottery-bound teams has reached unprecedented levels this season, exposing a structural problem that is fundamentally altering the competitive landscape of the NBA, <a href="https://sports.yahoo.com/nba/article/welcome-to-the-nbas-new-a-league-and-b-league-phenomenon-174126322.html">according to a report by Yahoo's Tom Haberstroh</a>. The combined gap between the No. 10 and No. 11 seeds in each conference has ballooned to 20.5 games, nearly double the previous record this century.</p>
<p>In the Eastern Conference, the No. 10-seeded Charlotte Hornets hold a 9.5-game cushion over the No. 11 Milwaukee Bucks. Out West, the Golden State Warriors lead the Memphis Grizzlies by 11 games for the final play-in spot.</p>
<p>The numbers are historical as the previous largest combined gap this century stood at 11 games in 2018-19. This season's 20.5-game chasm represents by far the widest separation since the NBA adopted its 16-team playoff format in 1984.</p>
<p>The play-in tournament, introduced as an anti-tanking measure, appears to have accelerated the problem rather than solved it. In the first full 82-game season following its introduction, the combined gap between No. 10 and No. 11 seeds was seven games. It has climbed steadily since, reaching nine games in 2022-23, nine again in 2023-24 and ten in 2024-25, before this season's dramatic surge. The average gap in the play-in era is 11 games, compared to just 4.8 games across the previous ten seasons.</p>
<p>Nine teams currently operate as de facto non-competitors, posting a combined 12-167 record against playoff-caliber opponents across recent stretches of play. In March alone, those nine teams have gone 12-98, a .109 winning percentage, against postseason contenders.</p>
<p>The Washington Wizards have lost 20 consecutive games against winning teams. The Brooklyn Nets are 1-29 in its last 30 such contests. Dallas stands at 2-23 in its last 25.</p>
<p>Two factors are widely cited as accelerants. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/lottery_winners">The leaps by the Dallas Mavericks from the No. 11 lottery position to the No. 1 overall pick in 2025 and the Atlanta Hawks from 10th in consecutive recent drafts demonstrated that long-shot lottery odds can pay off spectacularly</a>. Combined with a strong 2026 draft class, the cost-benefit calculation for struggling franchises increasingly favors chasing lottery balls over competing for a play-in berth. Only one of ten No. 10 seeds in the play-in era has successfully advanced to the full playoffs.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284708/NBA-Presented-Three-Draft-Lottery-Reform-Options-To-Board-Of-Governors">The NBA is proposing three potential lottery reforms in an attempt to disincentivize tanking</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 15:49:52 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284839/NBAs-Gap-Between-Playoff-Lottery-Teams-Reaches-Historic-Levels-Exposing-Two-League-Problems</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Young_Trae_was_260306.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Knicks, Wizards Among Teams That Tried To Trade Up For Jaden Ivey At 2022 NBA Draft</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284838/Knicks-Wizards-Among-Teams-That-Tried-To-Trade-Up-For-Jaden-Ivey-At-2022-NBA-Draft</link>
      <description><p>The New York Knicks and Washington Wizards were among multiple teams that aggressively pursued Jaden Ivey at the 2022 NBA Draft, according to league sources. Detroit's phones were flooded with trade inquiries centered on the fifth overall pick before the Pistons ultimately kept him.</p>
<p>Detroit elected to stay put and select Ivey despite the volume of interest. The Pistons then acquired center Jalen Duren later in the lottery via trade, securing both targets on draft night.</p>
<p>There had been internal consideration that Ivey could be moved at the draft, sources said, but the front office under then-general manager Troy Weaver found a way to retain both prospects. Weaver viewed Duren as his primary objective entering the draft and structured his draft-night maneuvering around landing the Memphis center regardless of outcome.</p>
<p>At the time, the Sacramento Kings received considerable consternation for selecting Keegan Murray over Ivey. Murray has become a solid two-way player for the Kings and <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/282314/Keegan-Murray-Kings-Agree-To-Five-Year-$140M-Extension">signed a five-year, $140 million rookie contract extension in October</a>. </p>
<p>The Pistons envisioned Ivey as the long-term backcourt complement to franchise cornerstone Cade Cunningham, a dynamic, high-energy foil to Cunningham's methodical style.       <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284831/Kennedy-Chandler-Jazz-Sign-Second-10-Day-Contract</link>
      <description><p>The Utah Jazz have signed guard Kennedy Chandler to a second 10-day contract.</p>
<p>Chandler, 6-0 and 170 pounds, has appeared in six games for Utah, posting averages of 14.2 points, 6.8 assists and 3.2 rebounds in 32.3 minutes per contest, including one start.</p>
<p>The 23-year-old spent the bulk of this season with the Delaware Blue Coats of the NBA G League, starting 41 of 42 appearances and averaging 17.0 points, 9.0 assists, 2.7 rebounds and 1.9 steals in 34.7 minutes per game.</p>
<p>The Dallas Mavericks selected Chandler 38th overall in the 2022 NBA Draft. He appeared in 36 games with the Memphis Grizzlies during the 2022-23 season before embarking on an extensive G League career spanning 153 games with four affiliate clubs, including stints with the Memphis Hustle, Long Island Nets, Raptors 905 and Delaware.</p>
<p>The Memphis native played one collegiate season at Tennessee in 2021-22, starting all 34 games and averaging 13.9 points, 4.7 assists and 2.2 steals.</p></description>

      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284829/Jason-Kidd-Calls-For-Mavs-To-Move-On-After-Mark-Cuban-Reignites-Luka-Doncic-Trade-Controversy</link>
      <description><p>Dallas Mavericks coach Jason Kidd suggested that Mark Cuban's podcast comments was an attempt to draw him into an ongoing clash between Cuban and current governor Patrick Dumont. At the core of the dispute is Cuban's belief that he holds less influence over the team's basketball operations <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/273835/Mark-Cuban-To-Control-Mavs-Basketball-Operations-Forevermore">than was promised when he sold the franchise in December 2023</a>.</p>
<p>Cuban reignited the Luka Doncic trade debate Tuesday on the Intersections podcast, expressing regret over the sale and criticizing the February 2025 deal. Three words specifically implicated Kidd.</p>
<p>"That doesn't justify it for our coach and our general manager to stand up and trade our best player," Cuban said.</p>
<p>Kidd confirmed he phoned Cuban after the podcast aired. Speaking roughly 90 minutes before tipoff on Tuesday, he was measured but direct about being thrust between his former and current bosses.</p>
<p>"The things that are going on between two owners is between the two owners," Kidd said. "I think we have a great owner in Patrick and he's going to give us every resource to build a championship team. Out of respect to Cuban, he helped me and my family. We won a championship together."</p>
<p>Kidd deflected further questions about his role in the Doncic trade and redirected toward Dallas's future around rookie Cooper Flagg.</p>
<p>"When are we going to move on?" Kidd said. "We're focused on the present and the future and we've got an incredible opportunity to build."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 12:36:10 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284829/Jason-Kidd-Calls-For-Mavs-To-Move-On-After-Mark-Cuban-Reignites-Luka-Doncic-Trade-Controversy</guid>

   <item>
      <title>NBA Europe Draws Billion-Dollar Bids From Over 120 Investors Ahead Of 2027 Launch</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284822/NBA-Europe-Draws-Billion-Dollar-Bids-From-Over-120-Investors-Ahead-Of-2027-Launch</link>
      <description><p>The NBA's proposed European league attracted substantial financial commitments after a Tuesday midnight ET deadline for non-binding proposals closed. Multiple bidders submitted investments of at least $1 billion, with several others meeting or surpassing the league's projected $500 million entry threshold, according to two sources with direct knowledge of the submissions.</p>
<p>Adam Silver's office reported "serious" interest from more than 120 prospective investors across the 12 target cities, which include London, Paris, Milan, Barcelona, Madrid, Rome, Athens, Istanbul, Berlin, Munich, Lyon and Manchester.</p>
<p>NBA Deputy Commissioner Mark Tatum released a statement addressing the response.</p>
<p>"The level of engagement and the scale of the bids reflect the marketplace's belief in our proposed model and the enormous, untapped potential for European basketball," Tatum said. "We will now review the bids in more detail and shortlist the partners who share our vision and commitment to accelerating the growth of the game across the continent."</p>
<p>Established EuroLeague clubs were among the bidders, a development carrying considerable consequences for the continent's premier competition. Existing clubs seeking entry must pay the same license fee as any outside investor, with no guaranteed access.</p>
<p>Previously reported bidders include Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund for a London franchise, Qatar Sports Investments for Paris, and RedBird Capital, owner of AC Milan, for Milan.</p>
<p>A third source cautioned that European bidders are scrutinizing the NBA's proposed business model and that difficult negotiations remain ahead. The NBA, advised by JPMorgan Chase and the Raine Group, plans a 16-team structure targeting an October 2027 launch.</p>
<p><a href="https://soccer.realgm.com/wiretap/11967/MLS-Valuations-Hit-$23-Billion-Collectively-But-Revenue-Divide-Widens">By comparison, the average MLS soccer club is now worth $767 million</a>. The NBA is essentially creating a basketball version of MLS in Europe with a franchise model instead of single club.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 11:40:48 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284822/NBA-Europe-Draws-Billion-Dollar-Bids-From-Over-120-Investors-Ahead-Of-2027-Launch</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Silver_Adam_nba_240515.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA Draws Huge Interest In Proposed European League</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284819/NBA-Draws-Huge-Interest-In-Proposed-European-League</link>
      <description><p>The NBA has drawn huge interest in their proposed European league. Reports are that multiple bidders submitted investment proposals of at least $1 billion. The NBA's expected range was at least $500 million and several others met or exceeded that marker as well.</p>
<p>All told, the league received interest from 120 prospective partners.</p>
<p>NBA deputy commissioner Mark Tatum, who is overseeing the project, said that the level of interest backs the league's belief that an NBA European league is viable.</p>
<p>"We have received significant interest from a range of prospective teams and investors for permanent franchise spots in a new league in Europe."</p>
<p>"The level of engagement and the scale of the bids reflect the marketplace's belief in our proposed model and the enormous, untapped potential for European basketball," Tatum said. "We will now review the bids in more detail and shortlist the partners who share our vision and commitment to accelerating the growth of the game across the continent."</p>
<p>The NBA is known to be targeting 12 cities for the league. Those cities are: London and Manchester England; Paris and Lyon, France; Rome and Milan, Italy; Barcelona and Madrid, Spain; Berlin and Munich, Germany; Athens, Greece and Istanbul, Turkey.</p>
<p>There has also been recent reporting that NBA and the existing EuroLeague could form some sort of partnership. Former NBA executive Chus Bueno was named as the CEO of the EuroLeague. Bueno and the NBA have had a good working relationship, which could help a partnership between the two leagues to move forward.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 09:26:29 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284819/NBA-Draws-Huge-Interest-In-Proposed-European-League</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_realgm.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>J.B. Bickerstaff After Pistons Clinch First Division Title In 18 Years: 'We Expect More'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284815/JB-Bickerstaff-After-Pistons-Clinch-First-Division-Title-In-18-Years-We-Expect-More</link>
      <description><p>The Detroit Pistons clinched their first division title since the 2007-08 season following a 127-116 win over the Toronto Raptors on Tuesday night.</p>
<p>"You take pride in understanding how hard it is to do these things in this league," coach J.B. Bickerstaff said after the win. "You take pride in the fact that you have a group of young men who have grown and achieved certain milestones.</p>
<p>"It's a part of the process, and we expect more."</p>
<p>Jalen Duren finished the game with 31 points on 12-13 shooting. </p>
<p>Daniss Jenkins, who had 21 points and 5 assists, echoed Bickerstaff's sentiment.</p>
<p>"We knew we were trying to do something special this year," he said. "It's great to accomplish something like this, but we have our eyes on the playoffs and doing some damage there. We'll celebrate this tonight and get back to work tomorrow."</p>
<p>Two years after going 14-48, the Pistons are 55-21 on the season. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 02:35:20 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284815/JB-Bickerstaff-After-Pistons-Clinch-First-Division-Title-In-18-Years-We-Expect-More</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Bickerstaff_JB_det_25027.jpg" />

      <title>Candace Parker, Elena Delle Donne, Amar'e Stoudemire Headline 2026 HOF</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284814/Candace-Parker-Elena-Delle-Donne-Amare-Stoudemire-Headline-2026-HOF</link>
      <description><p>Candace Parker, Elena Delle Donne, Amar'e Stoudemire, and Doc Rivers will be inducted into the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame for the Class of 2026, according to Shams Charania of ESPN.</p>
<p>Parker, who won WNBA titles with three different teams, is the only player to win Rookie of the Year and MVP in the same season.</p>
<p>Delle Donne is also a two-time league MVP and won a title in 2019 with the Mystics.</p>
<p>Rivers is eighth all-time in coaching wins and won a title with the Celtics in 2008.</p>
<p>Stoudemire was a six-time All-Star and the 2003 Rookie of the Year.</p>
<p>The formal ceremonies for the Hall of Fame are scheduled in August. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 02:29:18 EDT</pubDate>

      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_realgm.jpg" />

   <item>
      <title>Jaden Ivey To Receive Full Salary Despite Being Waived By Bulls For Conduct Issues</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284812/Jaden-Ivey-To-Receive-Full-Salary-Despite-Being-Waived-By-Bulls-For-Conduct-Issues</link>
      <description><p>Jaden Ivey will collect his entire $10.1 million salary this season despite the Chicago Bulls releasing the guard for conduct detrimental to the team, sources briefed on the matter confirmed. Ivey, 24, will become an unrestricted free agent once he clears waivers.</p>
<p>In the days before his release, Ivey made a series of comments during Instagram livestreams, calling Catholicism "a false religion" and stating that God would not hear prayers from sinners. On Monday, he criticized Pride Month celebrations, including those organized by the NBA, characterizing them as representing "unrighteousness."</p>
<p>Bulls head coach Billy Donovan addressed the decision publicly.</p>
<p>"I think there's a certain level of standards and expectations that are here," Donovan said. "We have people from all different walks of life working in the building and players from all different walks of life. We've got to all be professional. I think there's got to be a high level of respect for one another, and we got to help each other and then be accountable to those standards."</p>
<p>Ivey was finishing the final year of a four-year rookie contract, having been selected fifth overall by the Detroit Pistons in the 2022 NBA Draft. Chicago acquired him in February via a three-team trade deadline deal that sent Kevin Huerter and Dario Saric to Detroit and Mike Conley to the Bulls.</p>
<p>Ivey appeared in just four games for Chicago and had not played since February 11. The team shut him down last week due to a sore left knee.</p>
<p>Despite the public explanation for his release, Ivey was waived through the standard waiver process and may sign with any NBA franchise once he clears.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 31 Mar 2026 23:10:13 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284812/Jaden-Ivey-To-Receive-Full-Salary-Despite-Being-Waived-By-Bulls-For-Conduct-Issues</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Ivey_Jaden_det_221114.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Stephen Curry Participates In Full Scrimmage, Eyes Sunday Return</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284811/Stephen-Curry-Participates-In-Full-Scrimmage-Eyes-Sunday-Return</link>
      <description><p>Golden State Warriors guard Stephen Curry received medical clearance to participate in a five-on-five scrimmage Tuesday, marking the most significant milestone in his recovery from a persistent right knee injury. A potential return Sunday against the Houston Rockets is now the target if he continues to progress.</p>
<p>Curry spoke candidly about his situation after the workout.</p>
<p>"It felt great," Curry told ESPN. "I'm checking the boxes. But with this, it's always unpredictable because I don't know how the knee will respond because it hasn't responded well in the past attempts to get to this five-on-five level. I'm right where I need to be for right now and I'm hoping that it continues until the weekend."</p>
<p>Steve Kerr offered a measured response to the development.</p>
<p>"Gotta see how [his body] responds to the scrimmage and we'll take it from there," Kerr said.</p>
<p>Curry will miss his 26th and 27th straight games Wednesday against the San Antonio Spurs and Thursday against the Rockets before Sunday's earliest possible return date.</p>
<p>The injury originated after a workout in Minneapolis on January 24. Curry briefly played through discomfort before exiting Golden State's home loss to the Detroit Pistons on January 30 and has not appeared since. An expected return following the All-Star break was delayed after his knee reacted poorly to on-court work in Los Angeles.</p>
<p>Lead medical decision maker Rick Celebrini held Curry out of contact work for weeks, clearing him only Tuesday morning for live action.</p>
<p>Golden State sits 36-39, currently holding the 10th seed in the Western Conference after losing 16 of their last 25 games. The franchise hopes to get Curry sufficient playing time before the play-in tournament, where two consecutive victories would secure the eighth playoff seed.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 31 Mar 2026 23:06:15 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284811/Stephen-Curry-Participates-In-Full-Scrimmage-Eyes-Sunday-Return</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Curry_Stephen_gsw_220523.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Lucas Williamson, Grizzlies Sign 10-Day Contract</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284810/Lucas-Williamson-Grizzlies-Sign-10-Day-Contract</link>
      <description><p>Wing Lucas Williamson has signed a 10-day contract with the Memphis Grizzlies.</p>
<p>Williamson (6-4, 205) has appeared in 46 games (15 starts) for the Memphis Hustle and Windy City Bulls during the 2025-26 NBA G League Tip-Off Tournament and regular season has averaged 7.2 points, 2.3 rebounds, 1.3 assists and 1.1 steals in 23.7 minutes. The 27-year-old has competed in 139 NBA G League games (46 starts) over four seasons with the Ontario Clippers, Hustle and Bulls. Williamson has a career PER of 7.9 in the G League.</p>
<p>Williamson played five years at Loyola Chicago, where he was named the Missouri Valley Conference Defensive Player of the Year twice (2021, 2022) and was a member of the Ramblers' 2018 NCAA Final Four team. He went unselected in the 2022 NBA Draft.</p>
<p>Williamson is represented by <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/info/agent_clients/Keith-Kreiter/153">Keith Kreiter</a> and <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/info/agent_clients/Sam-Cipriano/782">Sam Cipriano</a>.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Sources Detail LeBron James' Most Likely Landing Spots For 2026-27 Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284809/Sources-Detail-LeBron-James-Most-Likely-Landing-Spots-For-2026-27-Season</link>
      <description><p>LeBron James faces a pivotal free agency decision this summer, with multiple teams emerging as realistic destinations for what could be his 24th NBA season. ESPN spoke with more than a dozen league sources to outline the leading options for the 41-year-old.</p>
<p>The Los Angeles Lakers will carry roughly $50 million in projected cap space with James' $52.6 million salary off the books. Sources told ESPN the franchise would welcome a return, though the salary James seeks will heavily influence the outcome.</p>
<p>The Cleveland Cavaliers represent what one Eastern Conference executive called the <br />"cleanest fit." Multiple league and Cavs sources told ESPN that if James wants a third stint, the franchise will find a way to make it work. A sign-and-trade involving Jarrett Allen could allow James to earn significant money while giving the Lakers a needed frontcourt piece.</p>
<p>"I always thought it made the most sense for him to go back home," a West scout said.</p>
<p>The Golden State Warriors offer a different appeal. James and Stephen Curry developed what coach Steve Kerr described as a "bromance" during the 2024 Paris Olympics. The Warriors also feature close James ally Draymond Green.</p>
<p>The Denver Nuggets drew perhaps the strongest basketball endorsement from sources. Teaming James with three-time MVP Nikola Jokic drew this assessment from one Western Conference executive: "Who is the only guy on [James'] level from a basketball IQ standpoint in the league? Go there and team up with that guy."</p>
<p>The New York Knicks surfaced as another possibility. James has repeatedly called Madison Square Garden his favorite arena, and the Knicks' front office has deep personal ties to him. Leon Rose was once James' agent, while William Wesley has had a personal connection since James was a teenager. However, a team source acknowledged the franchise must weigh disrupting its established core.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 31 Mar 2026 16:12:30 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284809/Sources-Detail-LeBron-James-Most-Likely-Landing-Spots-For-2026-27-Season</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/James_LeBron_lal_240202.jpg" />
   </item>
